meta.title: iAAi Beta Test — 12 University Benchmark Scores

universities.name: Stanford University
universities.golden: Stanford's people-oriented, project-management approach is the natural home for iAAi in the US market.

universities.name: MIT
universities.golden: MIT students would want to IMPROVE the game mechanics — which is the highest form of engagement.

universities.name: Khalifa University
universities.golden: A nation literally building itself from desert needs the civilisational perspective that iAAi provides.

universities.name: National University of Singapore
universities.golden: Singapore's Smart Nation initiative and Liveable Cities research align naturally with iAAi's systems thinking.

universities.name: University of Cambridge
universities.golden: Cambridge's cross-disciplinary philosophy is more aligned with iAAi than any traditional civil-only programme.

universities.name: King Fahd University
universities.golden: KFUPM sits at the nexus of petroleum infrastructure and Vision 2030 — iAAi provides the historical depth that forward-looking curricula lack.

universities.name: UC Berkeley
universities.golden: Berkeley students would critique the platform's framing — this is not a weakness, it is an opportunity.

universities.name: Qatar University
universities.golden: Qatar's $200B+ World Cup infrastructure investment shaped the university curriculum — iAAi provides the historical mirror.

universities.name: Imperial College London
universities.golden: The platform fills a gap that Imperial's technical focus deliberately excludes.

universities.name: Tsinghua University
universities.golden: The platform is more useful for Western students learning about the East than for Eastern students learning about themselves.

universities.name: IIT Bombay
universities.golden: India arguably represents a fourth mode — the Syncretic.

universities.name: University of Nottingham
universities.golden: Alignment is at the level of inspiration, not instruction.
